SignedHeaders=content-type;host;x-amz-content-sha256;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token;x-amz-user-agent, occurs\",\n142\t\t\t\tvalidateHeaders: []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\"},\n143\t\t\t},\n144\t\t}\n145\t\n146\t\tt.Log(\"Testing Multi-Hop Header Preservation\")\n147\t\tt.Log(\"======================================\")\n148\t\tt.Log(\"This test simulates requests passing through multiple intermediate\")\n149\t\tt.Log(\"proxies (Cloudflare, load balancers, etc.) before reaching ARMOR.\")\n150\t\tt.Log(\"It verifies that authentication headers survive intact through all hops.\")\n151\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n152\t\n153\t\tfor _, scenario := range testScenarios {\n154\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n155\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Scenario: %s\", scenario.description)\n156\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Hops: %d\", len(scenario.hops))\n157\t\n158\t\t\t\t// Build the handler chain: hops → ARMOR\n159\t\t\t\thandler := armorHandler\n160\t\t\t\tfor i := len(scenario.hops) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n161\t\t\t\t\thandler = scenario.hops[i](handler)\n162\t\t\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\t\t\tfor _, tc := range headerTestCases {\n165\t\t\t\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n166\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n167\t\n168\t\t\t\t\t\t// Create request with authentication headers\n169\t\t\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n170\t\t\t\t\t\tfor key, value := range tc.headers {\n171\t\t\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n172\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n173\t\n174\t\t\t\t\t\t// Store original headers for comparison\n175\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n176\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n177\t\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[key] = req.Header.Get(key)\n178\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n179\t\n180\t\t\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n181\t\t\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n182\t\n183\t\t\t\t\t\t// Create a custom wrapper to capture headers at ARMOR's boundary\n184\t\t\t\t\t\tvar capturedHeadersAtArmor map[string]string\n185\t\t\t\t\t\tvar authCaptured bool\n186\t\n187\t\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n188\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Capture headers as they arrive at ARMOR (after all hops)\n189\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n190\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n191\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor[key] = r.Header.Get(key)\n192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n193\t\t\t\t\t\t\tauthCaptured = true\n194\t\n195\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Call the actual handler\n196\t\t\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n197\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n198\t\n199\t\t\t\t\t\t// Serve the request through the multi-hop chain\n200\t\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n201\t\n202\t\t\t\t\t\t// Verify we captured headers at ARMOR boundary\n203\t\t\t\t\t\tif !authCaptured {\n204\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at ARMOR boundary\")\n205\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n206\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n207\t\n208\t\t\t\t\t\t// Validate each critical header\n209\t\t\t\t\t\tallPassed := true\n210\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n211\t\t\t\t\t\t\toriginal := originalHeaders[key]\n212\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcaptured := capturedHeadersAtArmor[key]\n213\t\n214\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original == \"\" && captured == \"\" {\n215\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Header was not sent, skip validation\n216\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n217\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original != captured {\n220\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Header %q was corrupted during multi-hop passthrough!\", key)\n221\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original length:  %d bytes\", len(original))\n222\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured length:  %d bytes\", len(captured))\n223\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original value:   %q\", truncateForLog(original))\n224\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured value:   %q\", truncateForLog(captured))\n225\t\n226\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Find first difference\n227\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(original)\n228\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(captured) < minLen {\n229\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(captured)\n230\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n231\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n232\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original[i] != captured[i] {\n233\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  First difference at byte %d:\", i)\n234\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    original[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, original[i], original[i])\n235\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    captured[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, captured[i], captured[i])\n236\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n237\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n239\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tallPassed = false\n240\t\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n241\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Header %q preserved intact (%d bytes)\", key, len(captured))\n242\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n244\t\n245\t\t\t\t\t\tif allPassed {\n246\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All headers survived %d-hop journey intact\", len(scenario.hops))\n247\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n248\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n249\t\t\t\t\t})\n250\t\t\t\t}\n251\t\t\t})\n252\t\t}\n253\t\n254\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Multi-hop header preservation verified\")\n255\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Authorization and X-Amz-* headers survive through intermediate proxies\")\n256\t}\n257\t\n258\t// TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior tests multi-hop scenarios with more\n259\t// realistic proxy behavior that might strip or modify headers.\n260\tfunc TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior(t *testing.T) {\n261\t\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n262\t\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n263\t\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n264\t\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n265\t\t\t\tACLs: authHeader)\n378\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n379\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n380\t\n381\t\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR headers correctly\")\n408\t\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Load balancer preserves auth headers correctly\")\n411\t\t})\n412\t}\n413\t\n414\t// TestMultiHopEndToEndHeaderFidelity tests complete end-to-end header fidelity\n415\t// through the entire request path, simulating a real production environment.\n416\tfunc TestMultiHopEndToEndHeaderFidelity(t *testing.T) {\n417\t\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n418\t\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n419\t\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n420\t\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n421\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n422\t\t\t},\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\tcfg := &config.Config{\n426\t\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n427\t\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n428\t\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n429\t\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n430\t\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n431\t\t}\n432\t\n433\t\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n434\t\tif err != nil {\n435\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n436\t\t}\n437\t\n438\t\tarmorHandler := srv.Handler()\n439\t\n440\t\tt.Run(\"Complete production path: Client → Cloudflare → Load Balancer → ARMOR\", func(t *testing.T) {\n441\t\t\tt.Log(\"Testing complete production request path:\")\n442\t\t\tt.Log(\"1. Client creates S3 request with Authorization header\")\n443\t\t\tt.Log(\"2. Request passes through Cloudflare (CDN/reverse proxy)\")\n444\t\t\tt.Log(\"3. Request passes through load balancer (L7 LB)\")\n445\t\t\tt.Log(\"4. Request reaches ARMOR S3 API server\")\n446\t\t\tt.Log(\"5. Verify headers are byte-for-byte identical at each step\")\n447\t\n448\t\t\t// Build the complete proxy chain\n449\t\t\thandler := armorHandler\n450\t\n451\t\t\t// Layer 3: Load Balancer (closest to ARMOR)\n452\t\t\thandler = loadBalancerLayer(handler)\n453\t\n454\t\t\t// Layer 2: Cloudflare (in front of load balancer)\n455\t\t\thandler = cloudflareProxyLayer(handler)\n456\t\n457\t\t\t// Capture headers at each hop\n458\t\t\tvar (\n459\t\t\t\theadersAfterCF, headersAfterLB, headersAtArmor map[string]string\n460\t\t\t\tcapturedCF, capturedLB, capturedArmor bool\n461\t\t\t)\n462\t\n463\t\t\t// Wrap to capture after Cloudflare\n464\t\t\tcaptureAfterCF := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n465\t\t\t\theadersAfterCF = make(map[string]string)\n466\t\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n467\t\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n468\t\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n469\t\t\t\tcapturedCF = true\n470\t\n471\t\t\t\t// Continue to load balancer\n472\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n473\t\t\t})\n474\t\n475\t\t\t// Wrap to capture after Load Balancer\n476\t\t\tcaptureAfterLB := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n477\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB = make(map[string]string)\n478\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n479\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n480\t\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n481\t\t\t\tcapturedLB = true\n482\t\n483\t\t\t\t// Continue to ARMOR\n484\t\t\t\tcaptureAfterCF.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n485\t\t\t})\n486\t\n487\t\t\t// Wrap to capture at ARMOR boundary\n488\t\t\tcaptureAtArmor := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n489\t\t\t\theadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n490\t\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n491\t\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = ARMOR\n496\t\t\t\tcaptureAfterLB.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n497\t\t\t})\n498\t\n499\t\t\t// Create client request with full S3 auth\n500\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n501\t\t\toriginalAuth := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n502\t\t\toriginalAmzDate := \"20130524T000000Z\"\n503\t\t\toriginalAmzSha := \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\"\n504\t\n505\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", originalAuth)\n506\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", {\n516\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at all hop points\")\n517\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"capturedCF: %v, capturedLB: %v, capturedArmor: %v\", capturedCF, capturedLB, capturedArmor)\n518\t\t\t\treturn\n519\t\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t\t// Verify end-to-end fidelity\n522\t\t\tt.Log(\"Verifying end-to-end header fidelity:\")\n523\t\n524\t\t\tallHeaders := []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\", \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"}\n525\t\t\tallPreserved := true\n526\t\n527\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range allHeaders {\n528\t\t\t\tvar originalValue string\n529\t\t\t\tswitch headerName {\n530\t\t\t\tcase \"Authorization\":\n531\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAuth\n532\t\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Date\":\n533\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzDate\n534\t\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\":\n535\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzSha\n536\t\t\t\t}\n537\t\n538\t\t\t\tcfValue := headersAfterCF[headerName]\n539\t\t\t\tlbValue := headersAfterLB[headerName]\n540\t\t\t\tarmorValue := headersAtArmor[headerName]\n541\t\n542\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  %s:\", headerName)\n543\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    Original: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(originalValue), len(originalValue))\n544\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After CF: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(cfValue), len(cfValue))\n545\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After LB: %q (len=%d)\", lbValue && lbValue == armorValue {\n549\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✓ Preserved through all hops\")\n550\t\t\t\t} else {\n551\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✗ CORRUPTED during passthrough!\")\n552\t\t\t\t\tallPreserved = false\n553\t\t\t\t}\n554\t\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t\tif allPreserved {\n557\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ End-to-end header fidelity verified\")\n558\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ All headers preserved byte-for-byte through complete production path\")\n559\t\t\t} else {\n560\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"✗ Header corruption detected in multi-hop path\")\n561\t\t\t}\n562\t\t})\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\t// Helper types and functions for multi-hop testing\n566\t\n567\t// hopLayer represents a middleware layer in the request path\n568\ttype hopLayer func(next http.Handler) http.Handler\n569\t\n570\t// cloudflareProxyLayer simulates Cloudflare as a reverse proxy\n571\t// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers but adds its own metadata headers\n572\tfunc cloudflareProxyLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n573\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n574\t\t\t// Cloudflare adds response headers\n575\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n576\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-Cache-Status\", \"MISS\")\n577\t\n578\t\t\t// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers (critical behavior)\n579\t\t\t// Cloudflare does NOT strip Authorization, X-Amz-* headers\n580\t\n581\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n582\t\t})\n583\t}\n584\t\n585\t// loadBalancerLayer simulates a layer 7 load balancer\n586\t// Load balancers typically preserve authentication headers for S3 API compatibility\n587\tfunc loadBalancerLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n588\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n589\t\t\t// Load balancers add forwarding headers\n590\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n591\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", \"https\")\n592\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Host\", \"armor.example.com\")\n593\t\n594\t\t\t// Load balancers preserve authentication headers (critical for S3 APIs)\n595\t\n596\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n597\t\t})\n598\t}\n599\t\n600\t// reverseProxyLayer simulates a generic reverse proxy (nginx, envoy, etc.)\n601\tfunc reverseProxyLayer(name string) hopLayer {\n602\t\treturn func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n603\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n604\t\t\t\t// Generic reverse proxy behavior\n605\t\t\t\t// Most reverse proxies preserve authentication headers by default\n606\t\t\t\t// unless explicitly configured to strip them\n607\t\n608\t\t\t\t// Add proxy identification header\n609\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-By\", name)\n610\t\n611\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n612\t\t\t})\n613\t\t}\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t// TestMultiHopHeaderIntegrityUnderLoad {\n\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[key] = req.Header.Get(key)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n\t\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Create a custom wrapper to capture headers at ARMOR's boundary\n\t\t\t\t\tvar capturedHeadersAtArmor map[string]string\n\t\t\t\t\tvar authCaptured bool\n\n\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Capture headers as they arrive at ARMOR (after all hops)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeadersAtArmor[key] = r.Header.Get(key)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tauthCaptured = true\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Call the actual handler\n\t\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Serve the request through the multi-hop chain\n\t\t\t\t\tfinalHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Verify we captured headers at ARMOR boundary\n\t\t\t\t\tif !authCaptured {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at ARMOR boundary\")\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate each critical header\n\t\t\t\t\tallPassed := true\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, key := range tc.validateHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\t\toriginal := originalHeaders[key]\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcaptured := capturedHeadersAtArmor[key]\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif original == \"\" && captured == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Header was not sent, skip validation\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif original != captured {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Header %q was corrupted during multi-hop passthrough!\", key)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original length:  %d bytes\", len(original))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured length:  %d bytes\", len(captured))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original value:   %q\", truncateForLog(original))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured value:   %q\", truncateForLog(captured))\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Find first difference\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(original)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(captured) < minLen {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(captured)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif original[i] != captured[i] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  First difference at byte %d:\", i)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    original[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, original[i], original[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    captured[%d] = %c (0x%02x)\", i, captured[i], captured[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tallPassed = false\n\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Header %q preserved intact (%d bytes)\", key, len(captured))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif allPassed {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All headers survived %d-hop journey intact\", len(scenario.hops))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Multi-hop header preservation verified\")\n\tt.Log(\"✓ Authorization and X-Amz-* headers survive through intermediate proxies\")\n}\n\n// TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior tests multi-hop scenarios with more\n// realistic proxy behavior that might strip or modify headers.\nfunc TestMultiHopWithRealisticProxyBehavior(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tarmorHandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Realistic Cloudflare proxy with header passthrough\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing realistic Cloudflare proxy behavior:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Cloudflare adds CF-RAY, CF-IPCountry headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Cloudflare preserves Authorization and X-Amz-* headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Request path: Client → Cloudflare → ARMOR\")\n\n\t\t// Create realistic Cloudflare proxy layer\n\t\trealisticCloudflare := func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t// Simulate Cloudflare adding its own headers\n\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-IPCountry\", \"US\")\n\n\t\t\t\t// Cloudflare preserves auth headers (this is the critical behavior)\n\t\t\t\t// In reality, Cloudflare does NOT strip Authorization or X-Amz-* headers\n\n\t\t\t\t// Call next handler\n\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thandler := realisticCloudflare(armorHandler)\n\n\t\t// Create request with full S3 auth headers\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR boundary\n\t\tvar capturedAuth, capturedAmzDate, capturedAmzSha string\n\t\tcapturingHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tcapturedAuth = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\tcapturedAmzDate = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\tcapturedAmzSha = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tcapturingHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify headers are preserved\n\t\tif capturedAuth != authHeader {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header corrupted by Cloudflare proxy\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Expected: %q\", truncateForLog(authHeader))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Got:      %q\", truncateForLog(capturedAuth))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif capturedAmzDate != \"20130524T000000Z\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header corrupted by Cloudflare proxy\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif capturedAmzSha != \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256 header corrupted by Cloudflare proxy\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Content-Sha256 header preserved through Cloudflare\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Realistic Cloudflare proxy preserves auth headers correctly\")\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Load balancer that preserves authentication headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing load balancer behavior:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Load balancer adds X-Forwarded-* headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Load balancer preserves Authorization and X-Amz-* headers\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Request path: Client → Load Balancer → ARMOR\")\n\n\t\t// Create realistic load balancer layer\n\t\tloadBalancer := func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t// Simulate load balancer adding forwarding headers\n\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", \"https\")\n\t\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Host\", \"armor.example.com\")\n\n\t\t\t\t// Load balancers typically preserve auth headers\n\t\t\t\t// This is critical for S3 API compatibility\n\n\t\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thandler := loadBalancer(armorHandler)\n\n\t\t// Create request with auth headers\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t// Capture headers at ARMOR boundary\n\t\tvar capturedAuth, capturedAmzDate string\n\t\tcapturingHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tcapturedAuth = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\tcapturedAmzDate = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tcapturingHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify headers are preserved\n\t\tif capturedAuth != authHeader {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization header corrupted by load balancer\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved through load balancer\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif capturedAmzDate != \"20130524T000000Z\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date header corrupted by load balancer\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ X-Amz-Date header preserved through load balancer\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify X-Forwarded headers were added\n\t\tif req.Header.Get(\"X-Forwarded-For\") != \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Load balancer added X-Forwarded headers correctly\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Log(\"✓ Load balancer preserves auth headers correctly\")\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestMultiHopEndToEndHeaderFidelity tests complete end-to-end header fidelity\n// through the entire request path, simulating a real production environment.\nfunc TestMultiHopEndToEndHeaderFidelity(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tarmorHandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Complete production path: Client → Cloudflare → Load Balancer → ARMOR\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing complete production request path:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"1. Client creates S3 request with Authorization header\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"2. Request passes through Cloudflare (CDN/reverse proxy)\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"3. Request passes through load balancer (L7 LB)\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"4. Request reaches ARMOR S3 API server\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"5. Verify headers are byte-for-byte identical at each step\")\n\n\t\t// Build the complete proxy chain\n\t\thandler := armorHandler\n\n\t\t// Layer 3: Load Balancer (closest to ARMOR)\n\t\thandler = loadBalancerLayer(handler)\n\n\t\t// Layer 2: Cloudflare (in front of load balancer)\n\t\thandler = cloudflareProxyLayer(handler)\n\n\t\t// Capture headers at each hop\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF, headersAfterLB, headersAtArmor map[string]string\n\t\t\tcapturedCF, capturedLB, capturedArmor bool\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// Wrap to capture after Cloudflare\n\t\tcaptureAfterCF := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterCF[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\tcapturedCF = true\n\n\t\t\t// Continue to load balancer\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Wrap to capture after Load Balancer\n\t\tcaptureAfterLB := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\theadersAfterLB[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\tcapturedLB = true\n\n\t\t\t// Continue to ARMOR\n\t\t\tcaptureAfterCF.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Wrap to capture at ARMOR boundary\n\t\tcaptureAtArmor := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor = make(map[string]string)\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"Authorization\"] = r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"X-Amz-Date\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\t\t\theadersAtArmor[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"] = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\")\n\t\t\tcapturedArmor = true\n\n\t\t\t// Call ARMOR\n\t\t\tcaptureAfterLB.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Create client request with full S3 auth\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\toriginalAuth := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\"\n\t\toriginalAmzDate := \"20130524T000000Z\"\n\t\toriginalAmzSha := \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\"\n\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", originalAuth)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", originalAmzDate)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", originalAmzSha)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t// Serve the complete request chain\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tcaptureAtArmor.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t// Verify we captured at all points\n\t\tif !capturedCF || !capturedLB || !capturedArmor {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture headers at all hop points\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"capturedCF: %v, capturedLB: %v, capturedArmor: %v\", capturedCF, capturedLB, capturedArmor)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify end-to-end fidelity\n\t\tt.Log(\"Verifying end-to-end header fidelity:\")\n\n\t\tallHeaders := []string{\"Authorization\", \"X-Amz-Date\", \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"}\n\t\tallPreserved := true\n\n\t\tfor _, headerName := range allHeaders {\n\t\t\tvar originalValue string\n\t\t\tswitch headerName {\n\t\t\tcase \"Authorization\":\n\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAuth\n\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Date\":\n\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzDate\n\t\t\tcase \"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\":\n\t\t\t\toriginalValue = originalAmzSha\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcfValue := headersAfterCF[headerName]\n\t\t\tlbValue := headersAfterLB[headerName]\n\t\t\tarmorValue := headersAtArmor[headerName]\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  %s:\", headerName)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    Original: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(originalValue), len(originalValue))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After CF: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(cfValue), len(cfValue))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    After LB: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(lbValue), len(lbValue))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    At ARMOR: %q (len=%d)\", truncateForLog(armorValue), len(armorValue))\n\n\t\t\tif originalValue == cfValue && cfValue == lbValue && lbValue == armorValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✓ Preserved through all hops\")\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    ✗ CORRUPTED during passthrough!\")\n\t\t\t\tallPreserved = false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allPreserved {\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ End-to-end header fidelity verified\")\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ All headers preserved byte-for-byte through complete production path\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"✗ Header corruption detected in multi-hop path\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// Helper types and functions for multi-hop testing\n\n// hopLayer represents a middleware layer in the request path\ntype hopLayer func(next http.Handler) http.Handler\n\n// cloudflareProxyLayer simulates Cloudflare as a reverse proxy\n// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers but adds its own metadata headers\nfunc cloudflareProxyLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t// Cloudflare adds response headers\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-RAY\", \"1234567890abcdef\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"CF-Cache-Status\", \"MISS\")\n\n\t\t// Cloudflare preserves authentication headers (critical behavior)\n\t\t// Cloudflare does NOT strip Authorization, X-Amz-* headers\n\n\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t})\n}\n\n// loadBalancerLayer simulates a layer 7 load balancer\n// Load balancers typically preserve authentication headers for S3 API compatibility\nfunc loadBalancerLayer(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t// Load balancers add forwarding headers\n\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-For\", \"203.0.113.1\")\n\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Proto\", \"https\")\n\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-Host\", \"armor.example.com\")\n\n\t\t// Load balancers preserve authentication headers (critical for S3 APIs)\n\n\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t})\n}\n\n// reverseProxyLayer simulates a generic reverse proxy (nginx, envoy, etc.)\nfunc reverseProxyLayer(name string) hopLayer {\n\treturn func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t// Generic reverse proxy behavior\n\t\t\t// Most reverse proxies preserve authentication headers by default\n\t\t\t// unless explicitly configured to strip them\n\n\t\t\t// Add proxy identification header\n\t\t\tr.Header.Set(\"X-Forwarded-By\", name)\n\n\t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMultiHopHeaderIntegrityUnderLoad tests header preservation when\n// multiple requests are processed concurrently through multi-hop paths.\nfunc TestMultiHopHeaderIntegrityUnderLoad(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tarmorHandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\t// Build multi-hop handler\n\thandler := armorHandler\n\thandler = loadBalancerLayer(handler)\n\thandler = cloudflareProxyLayer(handler)\n\n\tt.Run(\"Concurrent requests preserve headers correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tt.Log(\"Testing concurrent multi-hop requests:\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Multiple simultaneous requests through proxy chain\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Verify no cross-contamination between requests\")\n\t\tt.Log(\"- Verify each request's headers remain isolated and intact\")\n\n\t\tnumRequests := 10\n\t\terrors := make(chan error, numRequests)\n\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < numRequests; i++ {\n\t\t\tgo func(reqNum int) {\n\t\t\t\t// Create unique headers for this request\n\t\t\t\tuniqueSig := fmt.Sprintf(\"%064x\", reqNum) // 64-char hex signature\n\t\t\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", uniqueSig)\n\t\t\t\tamzDate := fmt.Sprintf(\"20130524T%06dZ\", reqNum)\n\n\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", amzDate)\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\")\n374\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n375\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n376\t\n377\t\t\toriginalSignedHeaders := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\")\n378\t\n379\t\t\tvar capturedSignedHeaders string\n380\t\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n381\t\t\t\tcapturedSignedHeaders = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\")\n382\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n383\t\t\t})\n384\t\n385\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n386\t\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n387\t\n388\t\t\tif len(capturedSignedHeaders) != len(originalSignedHeaders) {\n389\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders was truncated!\")\n390\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Expected length: %d\", len(originalSignedHeaders))\n391\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Got length:      %d\", len(capturedSignedHeaders))\n392\t\t\t} else {\n393\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ SignedHeaders preserved at %d characters with %d headers\",\n394\t\t\t\t\tlen(capturedSignedHeaders), headerCount)\n395\t\t\t}\n396\t\t})\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\t// TestHeaderDuplicationDetection verifies that headers are not duplicated\n400\t// or := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n638\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(tc.headerName, tc.headerValue)\n639\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n648\t\t\t\t\tcapturedValue = r.Header.Get(tc.headerName)\n649\t\t\t\t\tcapturedBytes = Byte-for-byte comparison\n657\t\t\t\tif capturedValue != originalValue {\n658\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Malformed header value was modified!\")\n659\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original bytes: %v\", originalBytes)\n660\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured bytes: %v\", capturedBytes)\n661\t\n662\t\t\t\t\t// Find differences\n663\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(originalBytes)\n664\t\t\t\t\tif len(capturedBytes) < minLen {\n665\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(capturedBytes)\n666\t\t\t\t\t}\n667\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n668\t\t\t\t\t\tif originalBytes[i] != capturedBytes[i] {\n669\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  First difference at byte %d:\", i)\n670\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    original[%d] = 0x%02x (%c)\", i, originalBytes[i], originalBytes[i])\n671\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    captured[%d] = 0x%02x (%c)\", i, capturedBytes[i], capturedBytes[i])\n672\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n673\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n674\t\t\t\t\t}\n675\t\t\t\t} else {\n676\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Malformed header preserved byte-for-byte (%d bytes)\", len(capturedBytes))\n677\t\t\t\t}\n678\t\t\t})\n679\t\t}\n680\t}\n681\t\n682\t// TestHeaderIntegrityWithEncodingVariations tests headers with various\n683\t// encoding schemes to ensure they're token\n\t\t\t1000, // Long token\n\t\t\t2000, // Very long token\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, length := range tokenLengths {\n\t\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Token length %d characters\", length), func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tlongToken := strings.Repeat(\"T\", length)\n\n\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Security-Token\", longToken)\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\")\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\t\t\toriginalToken := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Security-Token\")\n\n\t\t\t\tvar capturedToken string\n\t\t\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcapturedToken = r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Security-Token\")\n\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t\t})\n\n\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\t\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t\t\tif len(capturedToken) != len(originalToken) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Security token was truncated!\")\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Expected length: %d\", len(originalToken))\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Got length:      %d\", len(capturedToken))\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Security token preserved at %d characters\", len(capturedToken))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Long X-Amz-SignedHeaders not truncated\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create many signed headers\n\t\theaderCount := 20\n\t\tvar signedHeaders []string\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < headerCount; i++ {\n\t\t\tsignedHeaders = append(signedHeaders, fmt.Sprintf(\"x-custom-header-%d\", i))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\", Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n\n\t\toriginalSignedHeaders := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\")\n\n\t\tvar capturedSignedHeaders string\n\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\tif len(capturedSignedHeaders) != len(originalSignedHeaders) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders was truncated!\")\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Expected length: %d\", len(originalSignedHeaders))\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Got length:      %d\", len(capturedSignedHeaders))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ SignedHeaders preserved at %d characters with %d headers\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(capturedSignedHeaders), headerCount)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestHeaderDuplicationDetection verifies that headers are not duplicated\n// or multiplied during transit through ARMOR.\nfunc TestHeaderDuplicationDetection(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\thandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\tt.Run(\"Authorization header appears exactly once\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, \"FwoGZXIvYXdzEBYaDmRiwUKvH.example\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Algorithm\":       \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Amz-Credential\":      \"TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor make(map[string]int)\n\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tfor headerName := range testHeaders {\n\t\t\t\theaderCounts[headerName] = len(r.Header[headerName])\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\tallOnce := true\n\t\tfor headerName, count := range headerCounts {\n\t\t\tif count != 1 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Header %s appears %d times (expected 1)\", headerName, count)\n\t\t\t\tallOnce = false\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Header %s appears exactly once\", headerName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allOnce {\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ All X-Amz-* headers appear exactly once (no duplication)\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Multi-hop scenario preserves header count\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Build a multi-hop handler chain\n\t\tmultiHopHandler := handler\n\t\tmultiHopHandler = loadBalancerLayer(multiHopHandler)\n\t\tmultiHopHandler = cloudflareProxyLayer(multiHopHandler)\n\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", \"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\")\n\n\t\tvar finalAuthCount, finalAmzDateCount, finalAmzShaCount int\n\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\tfinalAuthCount = len(r.Header[\"Authorization\"])\n\t\t\tfinalAmzDateCount = len(r.Header[\"X-Amz-Date\"])\n\t\t\tfinalAmzShaCount = len(r.Header[\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\"])\n\t\t\tmultiHopHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\tallOnce := true\n\t\tif finalAuthCount != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Authorization appears %d times after multi-hop\", finalAuthCount)\n\t\t\tallOnce = false\n\t\t}\n\t\tif finalAmzDateCount != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Date appears %d times after multi-hop\", finalAmzDateCount)\n\t\t\tallOnce = false\n\t\t}\n\t\tif finalAmzShaCount != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256 appears %d times after multi-hop\", finalAmzShaCount)\n\t\t\tallOnce = false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allOnce {\n\t\t\tt.Log(\"✓ All headers preserved exactly once through multi-hop chain\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestHeaderIntegrityWithMalformedValues tests that malformed or unusual\n// header values are handled gracefully without corruption.\nfunc TestHeaderIntegrityWithMalformedValues(t *testing.T) {\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := &config.Config{\n\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n\t}\n\n\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\thandler := srv.Handler()\n\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\theaderName  string\n\t\theaderValue string\n\t\tdescription string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with trailing whitespace\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Test\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"value_with_trailing_spaces   \",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value with trailing spaces (preserved exactly)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with leading whitespace\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Test\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"   value_with_leading_spaces\",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value with leading spaces (preserved exactly)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with multiple spaces between words\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Test\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"word1    word2    word3\",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value with multiple spaces between words\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with tab characters\",\n\t\t\theaderName: endings\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Test\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"line1\\r\\nline2\\rline3\\nline4\",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value with mixed line endings (CRLF, CR, LF)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with only whitespace\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Test\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"    \\t\\t    \",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value containing only whitespace\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with repeated characters\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Test\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"AAAAAABBBBBCCCCCDDDDDEEEEE\",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value with repeated character sequences\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with alternating characters\",\n\t\t\theaderName: preserved)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Header with plus signs (URL encoding space)\",\n\t\t\theaderName:  \"X-Amz-Metadata-Plus\",\n\t\t\theaderValue: \"value+with+plus+signs+as+spaces\",\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Value with plus signs (URL-encoded spaces)\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n\n\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(tc.headerName, tc.headerValue)\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\")\n\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n\n\t\t\toriginalValue := req.Header.Get(tc.headerName)\n\t\t\toriginalBytes := []byte(originalValue)\n\n\t\t\tvar capturedValue string\n\t\t\tvar capturedBytes []byte\n\t\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\tcapturedValue = r.Header.Get(tc.headerName)\n\t\t\t\tcapturedBytes = []byte(capturedValue)\n\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t\t\t})\n\n\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\t\t\t// Byte-for-byte comparison\n\t\t\tif capturedValue != originalValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Malformed header value was modified!\")\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Original bytes: %v\", originalBytes)\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Captured bytes: %v\", capturedBytes)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Find differences\n\t\t\t\tminLen := len(originalBytes)\n\t\t\t\tif len(capturedBytes) < minLen {\n\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(capturedBytes)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n\t\t\t\t\tif originalBytes[i] != capturedBytes[i] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  First difference at byte %d:\", i)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    original[%d] = 0x%02x (%c)\", i, originalBytes[i], originalBytes[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"    captured[%d] = 0x%02x (%c)\", i, capturedBytes[i], capturedBytes[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Malformed header preserved byte-for-byte (%d bytes)\", len(capturedBytes))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestHeaderIntegrityWithEncodingVariations tests headers with various\n// encoding schemes to ensure they're preserved exactly.\nfunc Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request,SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date,Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\",\n93\t\t\t\texpectedAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n94\t\t\t\texpectedSignature: \"aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\",\n95\t\t\t\texpectedSignedHeaders: \"host;x-amz-date\",\n96\t\t\t\tdescription: \"Format with minimal spacing (no space after commas)\",\n97\t\t\t},\n98\t\t\t{\n99\t\t\t\tname: \"AWS4-HMAC-SMAC-SHA256 with extra spaces\",\n100\t\t\t\tauthHeader: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256  Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request,  SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, \"aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\",\n103\t\t\t\texpectedSignedHeaders: \"host;x-amz-date\",\n104\t\t\t\tdescription: \"Format with extra spaces (should be normalized)\",\n105\t\t\t},\n106\t\t}\n107\t\n108\t\tt.Log(\"Testing Authorization Header Passthrough to ARMOR\")\n109\t\tt.Log(\"This test verifies that Authorization headers are received intact without modification or corruption.\")\n110\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n111\t\n112\t\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n113\t\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n114\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n115\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original header: %s\", tc.authHeader)\n116\t\n117\t\t\t\t// Create request with the Authorization header\n118\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n119\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", tc.authHeader)\n120\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", \"20130524T000000Z\")\n121\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com\")\n122\t\n123\t\t\t\t// Store the original Authorization header for comparison\n124\t\t\t\toriginalAuth := req.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n125\t\n126\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n127\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n128\t\n129\t\t\t\t// Serve the request\n130\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n131\t\n132\t\t\t\t// Verify the response\n133\t\t\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK && w.Code != http.StatusForbidden && w.Code != http.StatusUnauthorized {\n134\t\t\t\t\t// We expect either success (if signature was valid), forbidden (if ACL denied), or unauthorized (if signature invalid)\n135\t\t\t\t\t// Any other status code indicates a problem with header parsing\n136\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Response status: %d\", w.Code)\n137\t\t\t\t}\n138\t\n139\t\t\t\t// Now verify the header was parsed correctly by attempting to parse it directly\n140\t\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalAuth)\n141\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n142\t\t\t\t\t// If parsing fails, this might be expected for malformed headers\n143\t\t\t\t\t// but for valid headers, this is an error\n144\t\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(tc.name, \"malformed\") && !strings.Contains(tc.name, \"invalid\") {\n145\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse Authorization header: %v\", err)\n146\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Header value: %s\", originalAuth)\n147\t\t\t\t\t}\n148\t\t\t\t\treturn\n149\t\t\t\t}\n150\t\n151\t\t\t\t// Verify the parsed components match expected values\n152\t\t\t\tif parsed.AccessKey != tc.expectedAccessKey {\n153\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey mismatch: expected %q, got %q\", tc.expectedAccessKey, parsed.AccessKey)\n154\t\t\t\t} else {\n155\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ AccessKey preserved: %q\", parsed.AccessKey)\n156\t\t\t\t}\n157\t\n158\t\t\t\tif parsed.Signature != tc.expectedSignature {\n159\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature mismatch: expected %q, got %q\", tc.expectedSignature, parsed.Signature)\n160\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Expected length: %d, Got length: %d\", len(tc.expectedSignature), len(parsed.Signature))\n161\t\t\t\t} else {\n162\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Signature preserved: %q (length: %d)\", parsed.Signature, len(parsed.Signature))\n163\t\t\t\t}\n164\t\n165\t\t\t\t// Verify signed headers\n166\t\t\t\texpectedHeaders := strings.Split(tc.expectedSignedHeaders, \";\")\n167\t\t\t\tif len(parsed.SignedHeaders) != len(expectedHeaders) {\n168\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count mismatch: expected %d, got %d\", len(expectedHeaders), len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n169\t\t\t\t} else {\n170\t\t\t\t\t// Compare signed headers as sets (order may vary)\n171\t\t\t\t\theaderMatch := true\n172\t\t\t\t\tfor _, expected := range expectedHeaders {\n173\t\t\t\t\t\tfound := false\n174\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, actual := range parsed.SignedHeaders {\n175\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif actual == expected {\n176\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n177\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n178\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n179\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n180\t\t\t\t\t\tif !found {\n181\t\t\t\t\t\t\theaderMatch = false\n182\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeader missing: %q\", expected)\n183\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n184\t\t\t\t\t}\n185\t\t\t\t\tif headerMatch {\n186\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ SignedHeaders preserved: %v\", parsed.SignedHeaders)\n187\t\t\t\t\t}\n188\t\t\t\t}\n189\t\n190\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Header passed through intact without truncation or modification\")\n191\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n192\t\t\t})\n193\t\t}\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t// TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughIntegration tests Authorization header passthrough\n197\t// through the full request handling pipeline.\n198\tfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n199\t\t// Create test credentials\n200\t\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n201\t\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n202\t\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n203\t\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n204\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil,\n205\t\t\t},\n206\t\t}\n207\t\n208\t\tcfg := &config.Config{\n209\t\t\tBucket:      \"test-bucket\",\n210\t\t\tB2Region:    \"us-east-005\",\n211\t\t\tCredentials: credentials,\n212\t\t\tMEK:         make([]byte, 32),\n213\t\t\tBlockSize:   65536,\n214\t\t}\n215\t\n216\t\tsrv, err := New(cfg)\n217\t\tif err != nil {\n218\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create server: %v\", err)\n219\t\t}\n220\t\n221\t\thandler := srv.Handler()\n222\t\n223\t\tt.Run(\"Real AWS SigV4 signed request preserves header\", func(t *testing.T) {\n224\t\t\t// Create a properly signed request using real AWS SigV4\n225\t\t\treq := createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n226\t\n227\t\t\t// Capture the Authorization header before sending\n228\t\t\toriginalAuth := req.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n229\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Sending request with Authorization header: %s\", truncateForLog(originalAuth))\n230\t\n231\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n232\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n233\t\n234\t\t\t// Serve the request\n235\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n236\t\n237\t\t\t// The request should be processed (may succeed or fail auth, but should not error on header parsing)\n238\t\t\tif w.Code == http.StatusBadRequest || w.Code == http.StatusInternalServerError {\n239\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Request failed with status %d, indicating possible header parsing issue\", w.Code)\n240\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Response body: %s\", w.Body.String())\n241\t\t\t}\n242\t\n243\t\t\t// Now parse the original header to verify it was well-formed\n244\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalAuth)\n245\t\t\tif err != nil {\n246\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse the Authorization header we just created: %v\", err)\n247\t\t\t\treturn\n248\t\t\t}\n249\t\n250\t\t\t// Verify all components are present\n251\t\t\tif parsed.Algorithm != \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\" {\n252\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Algorithm mismatch: expected AWS4-HMAC-SHA256, got %s\", parsed.Algorithm)\n253\t\t\t}\n254\t\n255\t\t\tif parsed.AccessKey != \"TESTACCESSKEY\" {\n256\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey mismatch: expected TESTACCESSKEY, got %s\", parsed.AccessKey)\n257\t\t\t}\n258\t\n259\t\t\tif parsed.Signature == \"\" {\n260\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature is empty (header may have been truncated)\")\n261\t\t\t}\n262\t\n263\t\t\tif len(parsed.SignedHeaders) == 0 {\n264\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders is empty (header may have been truncated)\")\n265\t\t\t}\n266\t\n267\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header preserved intact through request pipeline\")\n268\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Algorithm: %s\", parsed.Algorithm)\n269\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  AccessKey: %s\", parsed.AccessKey)\n270\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  Signature: %s (length: %d)\", truncateForLog(parsed.Signature), len(parsed.Signature))\n271\t\t\tt.Logf(\"  SignedHeaders: %v\", parsed.SignedHeaders)\n272\t\t})\n273\t}\n274\t\n275\t// TestAuthorizationHeaderEdgeCases tests edge cases for Authorization header handling\n276\tfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderEdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n277\t\tt.Run(\"Header with maximum length signature\", func(t *testing.T) {\n278\t\t\t// Create a header with a maximum-length signature (64 hex chars = 256 bits)\n279\t\t\tmaxSig := strings.Repeat(\"abcd\", 16) // 64 characters\n280\t\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", maxSig)\n281\t\n282\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n283\t\t\tif err != nil {\n284\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with max-length signature: %v\", err)\n285\t\t\t\treturn\n286\t\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\t\tif parsed.Signature != maxSig {\n289\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature truncated: expected length %d, got length %d\", len(maxSig), len(parsed.Signature))\n290\t\t\t}\n291\t\n292\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Maximum-length signature preserved: %d characters\", len(parsed.Signature))\n293\t\t})\n294\t\n295\t\tt.Run(\"Header with special characters in signature\", func(t *testing.T) {\n296\t\t\t// AWS signatures are hex, so test with all valid hex characters\n297\t\t\tspecialSig := \"abcdef0123456789ABCDEF0123456789abcdef0123456789ABCDEF0123456789\"\n298\t\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", specialSig)\n299\t\n300\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n301\t\t\tif err != nil {\n302\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with mixed-case signature: %v\", err)\n303\t\t\t\treturn\n304\t\t\t}\n305\t\n306\t\t\tif parsed.Signature != specialSig {\n307\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Special character signature not preserved: expected %q, got %q\", specialSig, parsed.Signature)\n308\t\t\t}\n309\t\n310\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Special character signature preserved\")\n311\t\t})\n312\t\n313\t\tt.Run(\"Header with many signed headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n314\t\t\t// Test with a large number of signed headers\n315\t\t\tmanyHeaders := []string{\"host\", \"x-amz-date\", \"x-amz-content-sha256\", \"content-type\", \"content-length\", \"x-amz-security-token\", \"x-amz-user-agent\"}\n316\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(manyHeaders, \";\")\n317\t\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=abc123\", signedHeadersStr)\n318\t\n319\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n320\t\t\tif err != nil {\n321\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with many signed headers: %v\", err)\n322\t\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t\t}\n324\t\n325\t\t\tif len(parsed.SignedHeaders) != len(manyHeaders) {\n326\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count mismatch: expected %d, got %d\", len(manyHeaders), len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n327\t\t\t}\n328\t\n329\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Many signed headers preserved: %d headers\", len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n330\t\t})\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing verifies that parsing and rebuilding\n334\t// the Authorization header produces the same result.\n335\tfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing(t *testing.T) {\n336\t\ttestHeaders := []string{\n337\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\",\n338\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20260714/us-west-2/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=content-type;host;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token, Signature=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef\",\n339\t\t}\n340\t\n341\t\tfor i, originalHeader := range testHeaders {\n342\t\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Round-trip test %d\", i+1), func(t *testing.T) {\n343\t\t\t\t// Parse the header\n344\t\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalHeader)\n345\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n346\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header: %v\", err)\n347\t\t\t\t\treturn\n348\t\t\t\t}\n349\t\n350\t\t\t\t// Reconstruct the header from parsed components\n351\t\t\t\t// The format is: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=..., SignedHeaders=..., Signature=...\n352\t\t\t\treconstructed := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s/%s/%s/%s/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=%s\",\n353\t\t\t\t\tparsed.AccessKey,\n354\t\t\t\t\tparsed.CredentialDate,\n355\t\t\t\t\tparsed.Region,\n356\t\t\t\t\tparsed.Service,\n357\t\t\t\t\tstrings.Join(parsed.SignedHeaders, \";\"),\n358\t\t\t\t\tparsed.Signature,\n359\t\t\t\t)\n360\t\n361\t\t\t\t// Parse the reconstructed header\n362\t\t\t\tparsedAgain, err := ParseAuthHeader(reconstructed)\n363\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n364\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse reconstructed header: %v\", err)\n365\t\t\t\t\treturn\n366\t\t\t\t}\n367\t\n368\t\t\t\t// Verify all components match\n369\t\t\t\tif parsedAgain.AccessKey != parsed.AccessKey {\n370\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.AccessKey, parsedAgain.AccessKey)\n371\t\t\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\t\t\tif parsedAgain.Signature != parsed.Signature {\n374\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.Signature, parsedAgain.Signature)\n375\t\t\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t\t\tif len(parsedAgain.SignedHeaders) != len(parsed.SignedHeaders) {\n378\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count changed during round-trip: %d -> %d\", len(parsed.SignedHeaders), len(parsedAgain.SignedHeaders))\n379\t\t\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header unchanged through parse/reconstruct cycle\")\n382\t\t\t})\n383\t\t}\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t// Helper function to truncate long strings for logging\n387\tfunc truncateForLog(s string) string {\n388\t\tif len(s) > 80 {\n389\t\t\treturn s[:40] + \"...\" + s[len(s)-37:]\n390\t\t}\n391\t\treturn s\n392\t}\n393\t\n394\t// TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughInStreamingMode tests Authorization header\n395\t// passthrough for streaming uploads (which use a slightly different auth format)\n396\tfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughInStreamingMode(t *testing.T) {\n397\t\tt.Run(\"Chunked streaming auth header format\", func(t *testing.T) {\n398\t\t\t// Streaming uploads use a slightly different format with the chunk signature\n399\t\t\t// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=..., SignedHeaders=..., Signature=..., x-amz-content-sha256=STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\n400\t\t\t// The actual chunk signatures come in subsequent headers\n401\t\n402\t\t\tauthHeader := \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date;x-amz-content-sha256, Signature=abc123\"\n403\t\n404\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n405\t\t\tif err != nil {\n406\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse streaming auth header: %v\", err)\n407\t\t\t\treturn\n408\t\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t\t// Verify x-amz-content-sha256 is in signed headers (required for streaming)\n411\t\t\tfound := false\n412\t\t\tfor _, h := range parsed.SignedHeaders {\n413\t\t\t\tif h == \"x-amz-content-sha256\" {\n414\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n415\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n416\t\t\t\t}\n417\t\t\t}\n418\t\n419\t\t\tif !found {\n420\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Streaming auth header missing x-amz-content-sha256 in signed headers\")\n421\t\t\t} else {\n422\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Streaming auth header format preserved correctly\")\n423\t\t\t}\n424\t\t})\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\t// TestAuthorizationHeaderExactPassthrough verifies expected := range expectedHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\tfound := false\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, actual := range parsed.SignedHeaders {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif actual == expected {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfound = handling pipeline.\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderPassthroughIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Create test credentials\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": truncateForLog(parsed.Signature), len(parsed.Signature))\n\t\tt.Logf(\"  SignedHeaders: %v\", parsed.SignedHeaders)\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestAuthorizationHeaderEdgeCases tests edge cases for Authorization header handling\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderEdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"Header with maximum length signature\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a header with a maximum-length signature (64 hex chars = 256 bits)\n\t\tmaxSig := strings.Repeat(\"abcd\", 16) // 64 characters\n\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", maxSig)\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with max-length signature: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif parsed.Signature != maxSig {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature truncated: expected length %d, got length %d\", len(maxSig), len(parsed.Signature))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Maximum-length signature preserved: %d characters\", len(parsed.Signature))\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Header with special characters in signature\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// AWS signatures are hex, so test with all valid hex characters\n\t\tspecialSig := \"abcdef0123456789ABCDEF0123456789abcdef0123456789ABCDEF0123456789\"\n\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\", specialSig)\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with mixed-case signature: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif parsed.Signature != specialSig {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Special character signature not preserved: expected %q, got %q\", specialSig, parsed.Signature)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Special character signature preserved\")\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"Header with many signed headers\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test with a large number of signed headers\n\t\tmanyHeaders := []string{\"host\", \"x-amz-date\", \"x-amz-content-sha256\", \"content-type\", \"content-length\", \"x-amz-security-token\", \"x-amz-user-agent\"}\n\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(manyHeaders, \";\")\n\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=abc123\", signedHeadersStr)\n\n\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header with many signed headers: %v\", err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(parsed.SignedHeaders) != len(manyHeaders) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count mismatch: expected %d, got %d\", len(manyHeaders), len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Many signed headers preserved: %d headers\", len(parsed.SignedHeaders))\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing verifies that parsing and rebuilding\n// the Authorization header produces the same result.\nfunc TestAuthorizationHeaderNotModifiedDuringParsing(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestHeaders := []string{\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=aeeed9bbccd4d02ee5c0109b86d86835f995330da4c265957d157751f604d404\",\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20260714/us-west-2/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=content-type;host;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token, Signature=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, originalHeader := range testHeaders {\n\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"Round-trip test %d\", i+1), func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Parse the header\n\t\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(originalHeader)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse header: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Reconstruct the header from parsed components\n\t\t\t// The format is: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=..., SignedHeaders=..., Signature=...\n\t\t\treconstructed := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s/%s/%s/%s/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=%s\",\n\t\t\t\tparsed.AccessKey,\n\t\t\t\tparsed.CredentialDate,\n\t\t\t\tparsed.Region,\n\t\t\t\tparsed.Service,\n\t\t\t\tstrings.Join(parsed.SignedHeaders, \";\"),\n\t\t\t\tparsed.Signature,\n\t\t\t)\n\n\t\t\t// Parse the reconstructed header\n\t\t\tparsedAgain, err := ParseAuthHeader(reconstructed)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to parse reconstructed header: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify all components match\n\t\t\tif parsedAgain.AccessKey != parsed.AccessKey {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"AccessKey changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.AccessKey, parsedAgain.AccessKey)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif parsedAgain.Signature != parsed.Signature {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Signature changed during round-trip: %s -> %s\", parsed.Signature, parsedAgain.Signature)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif len(parsedAgain.SignedHeaders) != len(parsed.SignedHeaders) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"SignedHeaders count changed during round-trip: %d -> %d\", len(parsed.SignedHeaders), len(parsedAgain.SignedHeaders))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header unchanged through parse/reconstruct cycle\")\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Helper function to truncate long strings for logging\nfunc truncateForLog(s string) string {\n\tif len(s) > 80 {\n\t\treturn s[:40] + \"...\" + s[len(s)-37:]\n\t}\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// originalAuth[i],\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, capturedAuthHeader[i], capturedAuthHeader[i])\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ Authorization header passed modification or corruption.\")\n237\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n238\t\n239\t\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n240\t\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n241\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Testing: %s\", tc.description)\n242\t\n243\t\t\t\t// Create request with the S3 headers\n244\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n245\t\n246\t\t\t\t// Set all the test headers\n247\t\t\t\tfor headerName, headerValue := range tc.headers {\n248\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(headerName, headerValue)\n249\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Setting %s: %s\", headerName, truncateForLog(headerValue))\n250\t\t\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\t\t\t// Store the original headers for comparison\n253\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n254\t\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range tc.validateHeaders {\n255\t\t\t\t\tif value := req.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n256\t\t\t\t\t\toriginalHeaders[headerName] = value\n257\t\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t\t}\n259\t\n260\t\t\t\t// Create a custom response recorder that captures the request\n261\t\t\t\tcapturedHeaders := make(map[string]string)\n262\t\t\t\tvar headersCaptured bool\n263\t\n264\t\t\t\t// Wrap the handler to capture the S3 headers as ARMOR receives them\n265\t\t\t\twrappedHandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n266\t\t\t\t\t// Capture all the S3 headers as ARMOR receives them\n267\t\t\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range tc.validateHeaders {\n268\t\t\t\t\t\tif value := r.Header.Get(headerName); value != \"\" {\n269\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcapturedHeaders[headerName] = value\n270\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n271\t\t\t\t\t}\n272\t\t\t\t\theadersCaptured = true\n273\t\n274\t\t\t\t\t// Call the original handler\n275\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n276\t\t\t\t})\n277\t\n278\t\t\t\t// Create response recorder\n279\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n280\t\n281\t\t\t\t// Serve the request through our wrapped handler\n282\t\t\t\twrappedHandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n283\t\n284\t\t\t\t// Verify we captured the S3 headers\n285\t\t\t\tif !headersCaptured {\n286\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Failed to capture S3 headers from request\")\n287\t\t\t\t\treturn\n288\t\t\t\t}\n289\t\n290\t\t\t\t// Verify each captured header matches the original exactly (byte-for-byte)\n291\t\t\t\tallMatched := true\n292\t\t\t\tfor _, headerName := range tc.validateHeaders {\n293\t\t\t\t\toriginalValue, originalExists := originalHeaders[headerName]\n294\t\t\t\t\tcapturedValue, capturedExists := capturedHeaders[headerName]\n295\t\n296\t\t\t\t\tif !originalExists {\n297\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Original header %s was not set\", headerName)\n298\t\t\t\t\t\tallMatched = false\n299\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n300\t\t\t\t\t}\n301\t\n302\t\t\t\t\tif !capturedExists {\n303\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Captured header %s is missing\", headerName)\n304\t\t\t\t\t\tallMatched = false\n305\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n306\t\t\t\t\t}\n307\t\n308\t\t\t\t\tif capturedValue != originalValue {\n309\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s header was modified during passthrough!\", headerName)\n310\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original length: %d\", len(originalValue))\n311\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured length: %d\", len(capturedValue))\n312\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Original:  %q\", originalValue)\n313\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Captured: %q\", capturedValue)\n314\t\n315\t\t\t\t\t\t// Find the first difference\n316\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen := len(originalValue)\n317\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(capturedValue) < minLen {\n318\t\t\t\t\t\t\tminLen = len(capturedValue)\n319\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n320\t\t\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < minLen; i++ {\n321\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif originalValue[i] != capturedValue[i] {\n322\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"First difference at byte %d: original[%d]=%c (0x%02x), captured[%d]=%c (0x%02x)\",\n323\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, i, originalValue[i], originalValue[i],\n324\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\ti, capturedValue[i], capturedValue[i])\n325\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n326\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n327\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n328\t\t\t\t\t\tallMatched = false\n329\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n330\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ %s preserved exactly (length: %d bytes)\", headerName, len(capturedValue))\n331\t\t\t\t\t}\n332\t\t\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\t\t\tif allMatched {\n335\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"✓ All S3 headers passed through intact (byte-for-byte match)\")\n336\t\t\t\t} else {\n337\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Some S3 headers were modified during passthrough\")\n338\t\t\t\t}\n339\t\t\t\tt.Log(\"\")\n340\t\t\t})\n341\t\t}\n342\t\n343\t\tt.Log(\"✓ S3-specific headers preservation test completed\")\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t//
